opportunities.title: Ostium Paused Trading Mid-Exploit — But the Oracle Was Broken Long Before the Multimillion-Dollar Bill Came Due
opportunities.why_now: A perp DEX just lost $18M+ to an oracle attack severe enough to force a full trading halt within hours of going live with the news.
opportunities.angle: Incident post-mortem: walk through the oracle manipulation mechanics and why 'pause trading' is the tell that the design was never adversarial-tested

opportunities.title: 'We'll Prioritize Security Going Forward' — The Most Predictable Sentence in Crypto
opportunities.why_now: Humanity Protocol's post-$36M-hack pledge to focus on ops security is the same statement issued after nearly every major exploit, and it's worth naming the pattern.
opportunities.angle: Pattern-recognition piece cataloguing the 'security-first now' promise across recent hacks (Humanity, SummerFi, Ostium) and why it always arrives too late

opportunities.title: ZachXBT Says Hardware Wallets Are 'Complete Garbage' — He's Not Wrong, He's Just Early to Saying It Out Loud
opportunities.why_now: A top on-chain investigator publicly trashing the industry's default cold-storage advice on the same day BTC chops sideways is a rare, quotable contrarian security take worth dissecting.
opportunities.angle: Reaction/analysis piece: what ZachXBT is actually pointing at (supply chain, firmware, seed exposure) versus what headline readers will assume he means

opportunities.title: SummerFi Didn't Get Hacked Into Extinction — It Got Exploited Into an Excuse to Quit
opportunities.why_now: A seven-year-old DeFi protocol is shutting down and blaming an exploit, a rare case where a project's death gives a clean window into how sustainable 'secure enough' actually was.
opportunities.angle: Obituary-style breakdown: was this exploit fatal on its own, or the final straw after years of accumulated technical debt no one priced in

opportunities.title: Tether Froze $131M in Iran-Linked Wallets in Minutes — So Much for 'Code Is Law'
opportunities.why_now: OFAC's sanctions action and Tether's near-instant freeze of the funds is a live demonstration that stablecoin 'ownership' is permissioned whenever Washington wants it to be.
opportunities.angle: Security/market-structure angle: what this freeze capability means for anyone treating USDT as bearer money, framed for readers who already assumed this was true
